Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search QuickBooks for the NorthWind invoice using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria referencing "NorthWind" or DocNumber "INV-2026-0412" or similar). The agent needs to find invoice INV-2026-0412 for NorthWind Technologies, TotalAmt $14,700, Balance $14,700 (unpaid), DueDate 2026-05-01, with 6 line items: (1) Standard Relocation Package, Raj Patel, Chicago IL → Denver CO, $4,500; (2) Hazmat Handling Surcharge, Lithium UPS Batteries (Class 9, DOT), $1,800; (3) Chain-of-Custody Security Protocol, IT Equipment (marked *** PROVISIONAL *** / first-time service), $1,200; (4) Standard Relocation Package, Emilia Cruz, Chicago IL → Denver CO, $4,500; (5) Specialty Piano Moving, Steinway Model B (climate-controlled + tuning, marked *** PROVISIONAL ***, pending subcontractor agreement and insurance), $2,200; (6) Early Lease Termination Negotiation Service (noted as non-standard MoveOps service, scope pending client confirmation), $500. Lines 3 and 5 are explicitly tagged PROVISIONAL, while line 6 is marked "pending client confirmation", all three are unsettled surcharges the prompt flags as suspicious.
- Search Airtable for NorthWind relocation records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001" or "base_moveops_ops", table_name: "Relocations", filter by Company: "NorthWind Technologies" or similar). The agent needs to find: (1) Raj Patel (recRajPatelChicagoDenver), Chicago → Denver, In Progress, April 21–23, 2026, coordinator Blessing Okafor, special requirements: home server lab with 4 rack-mounted servers (~2,400 lbs total), lithium UPS batteries (Class 9 hazmat, DOT-certified transport), CISO-mandated chain-of-custody log, NorthWind IT security escort must supervise all packing/loading, direct transport only (no interim warehouse), real-time GPS, full disk encryption verification; (2) Emilia Cruz (recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver), Chicago → Denver, In Progress, April 14–18, 2026, coordinator Blessing Okafor, special requirements: partner's Steinway Model B grand piano (7 ft, 480 lbs), specialty piano movers needed (Heartland and Swift do not handle pianos), potential subcontractor Gentle Giant Piano Movers (Chicago), NO existing vendor agreement (KeyMove Specialty Transport is also involved as discovered separately via emails and vendor bills, but is not listed in this Airtable record), 27-day lease overlap (Chicago lease ends May 15 but Denver needed by April 18), early lease termination negotiation; (3) Raj Patel audit note (recRajPatelAuditNote0411), In Progress, April 10, coordinator Fatimah Al-Rashidi, notes that movers arrived at 7:00 AM and loaded without waiting for the IT escort confirmed at 9:00 AM (chain-of-custody breach).
- Search emails for Emilia Cruz piano move issues using search_emails (query: "Emilia Cruz" or "Steinway" or "piano" or "KeyMove" or similar). The agent needs to discover: (a) Craig Nguyen from KeyMove Specialty Transport (craig.nguyen@keymove-specialty.com) emailed Blessing Okafor reporting that the Steinway Model B (satin ebony) suffered a scratch (~4.5–5 inches on the bass side rim near the keyboard cheek) during stairwell extraction at 2147 Humboldt St, Denver, on the move date of April 11; (b) Catalina Dubois sent David Chen an internal account update (email_catalina_david_northwind_update) describing both the Raj server lab and Emilia Cruz moves and their operational risks, this was a prospective planning email flagging potential issues before the incidents fully materialized; a separate draft email (email_email_ab99acca3399, folder: DRAFT, not sent) references "Victor's April 10 escalation" and "the Emilia Cruz damage issue" post-incident, showing Catalina acknowledged both failures but had not yet sent the follow-up; (c) David Chen emailed Elena Rostova about both incidents; (d) Pam Kowalski sent a final-warning email to David about the Raj breach and Emilia piano damage, indicating service recovery was not delivered. These emails confirm the piano surcharge of $2,200 on the NorthWind invoice is tied to a move that resulted in damage, which needs to be reconciled with the vendor claim.
- Search Slack for Emilia Cruz piano damage using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Steinway" or "piano" or "Emilia Cruz" or "KeyMove" or similar). The agent needs to discover: (a) Julian Brooks posted in #customer-support (C007) that Emilia called about the scratched Steinway and wants a callback about MoveOps vs KeyMove responsibility; (b) Blessing Okafor posted in #operations acknowledging the piano damage and that she should have required a site survey before the move; (c) Hana Kim (the persona) posted the INV-2026-0412 breakdown in #finance, noting the $1,200 chain-of-custody line as explicitly PROVISIONAL and the $2,200 piano and $1,800 hazmat lines as based on preliminary quotes that could shift once actual vendor contracts are in place.
- Search emails for Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach using search_emails (query: "Raj Patel" or "chain-of-custody" or "Victor Huang" or "server lab" or "SentinelChain" or similar). The agent needs to discover: (a) SentinelChain Logistics (dispatch@sentinelchain-logistics.com) sent a dispatch confirmation to Fatimah Al-Rashidi (fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com), cc Catalina Dubois (catalina.dubois@moveops.com), for Raj Patel pickup at NorthWind's server lab for Thursday, April 9, with IT escort confirmed at 9:00 AM, crew lead Miguel Herrera, vehicle Unit 14 / plate CGT-4189; (b) Victor Huang (NorthWind CISO, victor.huang@northwindtech.com) emailed Chloe Vance escalating that the crew started before the IT escort arrived, equipment was handled without authorization, and overnight warehouse storage occurred (both prohibited under the chain-of-custody protocol); (c) Victor Huang originally sent non-negotiable security requirements for 4 servers with proprietary source code to Catalina; (d) Catalina replied to Victor acknowledging all five security requirements and committing to verify with the vendor, stating "I'm not going to confirm we can deliver on them until I've verified it with the vendor" and promising a definitive answer by end of day with a draft chain-of-custody template, but the actual service violated these commitments. This confirms the $1,200 chain-of-custody surcharge is for a service that was not properly delivered.
- Search Slack for Raj Patel chain-of-custody details using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "chain-of-custody" or "Raj Patel" or "escort" or "SentinelChain" or similar). The agent needs to discover: (a) Chloe Vance posted a detailed timeline of the breach in #operations: 7:00 AM April 10 SentinelChain load-in (before 9:00 AM escort), 4:38 PM April 10 scan at South San Francisco warehouse overnight, 8:12 AM April 11 outbound, violating no pre-escort handling, chain-of-custody, and no interim storage requirements; (b) Discussion noting that the Linear DOT ticket (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind, #3) was marked "Done" with all items verified, but the actual custody log is missing; (c) Blessing Okafor mentioned that the Swift driver spent 45 minutes on the chain-of-custody section of the DOT packet for Emilia Cruz's household move (not the hazmat lab move). This creates a discrepancy: the DOT documentation may be marked complete but the actual chain-of-custody execution was botched.
- Search QuickBooks for the KeyMove vendor bill using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria referencing "KeyMove" or "piano" or "Emilia Cruz" or similar). The agent needs to find bill KM-44192-ICR from KeyMove Specialty Transport (VEND-KEYMOVE-001), TxnDate 2026-04-17, DueDate 2026-04-24, TotalAmt $1,200, Balance $1,200, description: "Insurance claim rider for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction", charged to Account ACC-6185 (Claims & Remediation Expense). This $1,200 vendor bill for the damage claim is separate from the $2,200 piano moving surcharge on the NorthWind invoice, and both need to be reconciled.
- Search QuickBooks for ALL April invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (broad query for April 2026 or by client names). The agent needs to find all invoices with surcharge lines to perform the full April surcharge audit. Key invoices and their surcharges: (a) INV-2026-0418 (GreenStack Energy, $9,440): hazmat transport $1,800, interim hazmat storage $1,650 (PENDING), overweight equipment $950, plus stipend platform $2,000; (b) INV-2026-0410 (Vectral Systems, $15,900): pet relocation surcharge $350, split-shipment $900, ADA housing premium $400, equipment stipend management $750; (c) INV-2026-0411 (Mosaic Robotics, $9,010): crating/climate surcharge $1,200, hazmat surcharge $800, interim storage surcharge $450, all PENDING CLIENT APPROVAL; (d) INV-2026-0308 (BrightLoop Analytics, $11,350): rush coordination surcharge $750, vehicle shipping $1,100, stipend platform fee $500; (e) INV-2026-0401 (Canopy Health, $9,210): TN visa/customs broker surcharge $850 (PENDING), cold-chain lab freight $1,200 (PENDING), temp storage $600 (PENDING), stipend platform $2,000; (f) INV-2026-0315 (PivotPoint Labs, $10,650): walkup surcharge $500, custom crating $1,200, climate freight $850, interim storage $640, cargo insurance $420.
- Search QuickBooks for vendor bills to cross-reference against invoice surcharges using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria matching April vendors like "Heartland", "Swift", "Atlas", etc.). The agent needs to find: (a) Heartland: HM-2026-0418 ($2,150 for Abdi move, $1,850 base + $175 special handling + $125 high-rise); HM-2026-Q1-0042 ($12,800 for 8 moves, Abdi, Tran, Kovac, Webb, Reeves, Marchetti, Osei, Venkatesh) and its HOLD variant HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD (same $12,800, but lines 6–8 marked DISPUTED, Marchetti cancelled, Osei cancelled, Venkatesh done by Swift); ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC ($90,000 damage accrual, DO NOT PAY); (b) Swift: SR-2026-0419 ($2,885 for Tran, $2,800 + $85 bike box), SR-2026-0421 ($4,450 for Venkatesh, $3,800 + $650 electronics), SR-2026-0422 ($3,675 for Kovac, $3,200 + $350 walkup + $125 electronics); (c) Atlas: ACT-2026-0414 ($439.40 for Andre Washington flight); (d) KeyMove: KM-44192-ICR ($1,200 piano claim, covered above). The agent cross-references these vendor bills with the corresponding invoice surcharge lines to confirm legitimacy.
- Search Airtable for ALL April relocation records using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records (table: "Relocations") to cross-check invoice surcharges against actual special requirements. The agent needs to verify that surcharges on each invoice align with the relocation record's special requirements: e.g., Priya Venkatesh's record confirms electronics surcharge ($650), Ines Kovac's record confirms walkup surcharge ($350), Kevin Tran's record confirms bike packing, Andre Washington's confirms 3D printer handling, Dr. Renata Kovacs confirms TN visa requirements, and so on. Any surcharge on an invoice that has no corresponding special requirement in the relocation record is a flag.
- Search Linear for existing tickets related to NorthWind and vendor issues using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "piano" or "chain-of-custody" or "KeyMove" or similar). The agent needs to find: (a) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind (#3), "DOT Docs, Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS", state: Done, assignee: Chloe Vance, description confirms Swift Relocations as carrier (PHMSA #3281074), 4 rack-mounted servers with lithium UPS, move window April 21–23, all checklist items marked VERIFIED including chain-of-custody/placarding; (b) linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 (#482), "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations", assignee: David Chen, references Victor Huang's server lab email, Chloe's timeline analysis, missing custody log, and Julian Brooks's note about Emilia Cruz's same-day callback request with no owner (piano damage details appear in Linear comments on this issue rather than in the issue description itself). The agent should note the contradiction: the DOT ticket is marked "Done" with chain-of-custody verified, but the actual execution had a chain-of-custody breach.
- Look up contact details for Marcus Thorne using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne" or "Marcus" or similar) to get marcus.thorne@moveops.com for the calendar event.
- Look up contact details for Catalina Dubois using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Catalina Dubois" or "Catalina" or similar) to get catalina.dubois@moveops.com for the calendar event.
- Correct QuickBooks entries based on audit findings. At minimum, the agent should flag or adjust the NorthWind invoice INV-2026-0412 surcharges: the $1,200 chain-of-custody charge is for a service that was not properly delivered (movers loaded before escort, equipment stored in prohibited warehouse overnight, confirmed by Victor Huang escalation and Chloe's timeline), and the $2,200 piano surcharge needs reconciliation against the $1,200 KeyMove insurance claim rider for the Steinway damage. The agent may create a corrected invoice (via quickbooks_create_invoice) or add notes/adjustments to reflect the audit findings. Any PENDING surcharges across other April invoices (GreenStack $1,650 storage, Mosaic $1,200/$800/$450, Canopy $850/$1,200/$600) should also be noted as requiring client approval before finalization.
- Update Airtable relocation records with audit findings using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01"). At minimum, the agent should update: (a) Emilia Cruz's record (recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver) with a note about the piano damage (Steinway scratched ~4.5–5 inches on bass side rim during stairwell extraction, KeyMove bill KM-44192-ICR filed for $1,200 insurance claim rider); (b) Raj Patel's record (recRajPatelChicagoDenver) with a note about the chain-of-custody breach (movers loaded at 7:00 AM before 9:00 AM escort, overnight warehouse storage, Victor Huang escalation, $1,200 surcharge under review).
- Create Linear tickets for unresolved vendor items using linear_create_issue. The agent should create at minimum: (a) A ticket for the KeyMove piano damage claim, the Steinway scratch needs resolution regarding MoveOps vs KeyMove liability, the $1,200 insurance claim rider has been billed but no resolution or settlement is documented; (b) A ticket for the SentinelChain chain-of-custody service failure, the breach (pre-escort loading, overnight warehouse storage) needs formal vendor follow-up and potential credit or surcharge reversal. Additional tickets may be warranted for pending surcharges requiring client approval across other April invoices.
- Schedule a calendar event on April 29, 2026 with Marcus Thorne and Catalina Dubois using calendar_add_calendar_event (start_datetime: "2026-04-29", attendees: marcus.thorne@moveops.com and catalina.dubois@moveops.com, title related to reviewing final NorthWind/April surcharge numbers before going to the client).
- Post audit findings in the #finance Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C005 or channel name "finance"). The message should summarize the surcharge audit results across all April invoices, highlighting: the NorthWind chain-of-custody surcharge issue ($1,200 for a botched service), the Emilia Cruz piano damage and $1,200 KeyMove insurance claim, PENDING surcharges across other clients requiring approval, and the April 29 meeting with Marcus and Catalina to finalize numbers.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
- Search QuickBooks for the NorthWind invoice using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria referencing "NorthWind" or DocNumber "INV-2026-0412" or similar). The agent needs to find invoice INV-2026-0412 for NorthWind Technologies, TotalAmt $14,700, Balance $14,700 (unpaid), DueDate 2026-05-01, with 6 line items: (1) Standard Relocation Package, Raj Patel, Chicago IL → Denver CO, $4,500; (2) Hazmat Handling Surcharge, Lithium UPS Batteries (Class 9, DOT), $1,800; (3) Chain-of-Custody Security Protocol, IT Equipment (marked *** PROVISIONAL *** / first-time service), $1,200; (4) Standard Relocation Package, Emilia Cruz, Chicago IL → Denver CO, $4,500; (5) Specialty Piano Moving, Steinway Model B (climate-controlled + tuning, marked *** PROVISIONAL ***, pending subcontractor agreement and insurance), $2,200; (6) Early Lease Termination Negotiation Service (noted as non-standard MoveOps service, scope pending client confirmation), $500. Lines 3 and 5 are explicitly tagged PROVISIONAL, while line 6 is marked "pending client confirmation", all three are unsettled surcharges the prompt flags as suspicious.

This confirms the $1,200 chain-of-custody surcharge is for a service that was not properly delivered.
- Search Slack for Raj Patel chain-of-custody details using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "chain-of-custody" or "Raj Patel" or "escort" or "SentinelChain" or similar). The agent needs to discover: (a) Chloe Vance posted a detailed timeline of the breach in #operations: 7:00 AM April 10 SentinelChain load-in (before 9:00 AM escort), 4:38 PM April 10 scan at South San Francisco warehouse overnight, 8:12 AM April 11 outbound, violating no pre-escort handling, chain-of-custody, and no interim storage requirements; (b) Discussion noting that the Linear DOT ticket (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind, #3) was marked "Done" with all items verified, but the actual custody log is missing; (c) Blessing Okafor mentioned that the Swift driver spent 45 minutes on the chain-of-custody section of the DOT packet for Emilia Cruz's household move (not the hazmat lab move). This creates a discrepancy: the DOT documentation may be marked complete but the actual chain-of-custody execution was botched.
- Search QuickBooks for the KeyMove vendor bill using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria referencing "KeyMove" or "piano" or "Emilia Cruz" or similar). The agent needs to find bill KM-44192-ICR from KeyMove Specialty Transport (VEND-KEYMOVE-001), TxnDate 2026-04-17, DueDate 2026-04-24, TotalAmt $1,200, Balance $1,200, description: "Insurance claim rider for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction", charged to Account ACC-6185 (Claims & Remediation Expense). This $1,200 vendor bill for the damage claim is separate from the $2,200 piano moving surcharge on the NorthWind invoice, and both need to be reconciled.
- Search QuickBooks for ALL April invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (broad query for April 2026 or by client names). The agent needs to find all invoices with surcharge lines to perform the full April surcharge audit. Key invoices and their surcharges: (a) INV-2026-0418 (GreenStack Energy, $9,440): hazmat transport $1,800, interim hazmat storage $1,650 (PENDING), overweight equipment $950, plus stipend platform $2,000; (b) INV-2026-0410 (Vectral Systems, $15,900): pet relocation surcharge $350, split-shipment $900, ADA housing premium $400, equipment stipend management $750; (c) INV-2026-0411 (Mosaic Robotics, $9,010): crating/climate surcharge $1,200, hazmat surcharge $800, interim storage surcharge $450, all PENDING CLIENT APPROVAL; (d) INV-2026-0308 (BrightLoop Analytics, $11,350): rush coordination surcharge $750, vehicle shipping $1,100, stipend platform fee $500; (e) INV-2026-0401 (Canopy Health, $9,210): TN visa/customs broker surcharge $850 (PENDING), cold-chain lab freight $1,200 (PENDING), temp storage $600 (PENDING), stipend platform $2,000; (f) INV-2026-0315 (PivotPoint Labs, $10,650): walkup surcharge $500, custom crating $1,200, climate freight $850, interim storage $640, cargo insurance $420.
- Search QuickBooks for vendor bills to cross-reference against invoice surcharges using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria matching April vendors like "Heartland", "Swift", "Atlas", etc.). The agent needs to find: (a) Heartland: HM-2026-0418 ($2,150 for Abdi move, $1,850 base + $175 special handling + $125 high-rise); HM-2026-Q1-0042 ($12,800 for 8 moves, Abdi, Tran, Kovac, Webb, Reeves, Marchetti, Osei, Venkatesh) and its HOLD variant HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD (same $12,800, but lines 6–8 marked DISPUTED, Marchetti cancelled, Osei cancelled, Venkatesh done by Swift); ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC ($90,000 damage accrual, DO NOT PAY); (b) Swift: SR-2026-0419 ($2,885 for Tran, $2,800 + $85 bike box), SR-2026-0421 ($4,450 for Venkatesh, $3,800 + $650 electronics), SR-2026-0422 ($3,675 for Kovac, $3,200 + $350 walkup + $125 electronics); (c) Atlas: ACT-2026-0414 ($439.40 for Andre Washington flight); (d) KeyMove: KM-44192-ICR ($1,200 piano claim, covered above). The agent cross-references these vendor bills with the corresponding invoice surcharge lines to confirm legitimacy.
- Search Airtable for ALL April relocation records using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records (table: "Relocations") to cross-check invoice surcharges against actual special requirements. The agent needs to verify that surcharges on each invoice align with the relocation record's special requirements: e.g., Priya Venkatesh's record confirms electronics surcharge ($650), Ines Kovac's record confirms walkup surcharge ($350), Kevin Tran's record confirms bike packing, Andre Washington's confirms 3D printer handling, Dr. Renata Kovacs confirms TN visa requirements, and so on. Any surcharge on an invoice that has no corresponding special requirement in the relocation record is a flag.
- Search Linear for existing tickets related to NorthWind and vendor issues using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "piano" or "chain-of-custody" or "KeyMove" or similar). The agent needs to find: (a) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind (#3), "DOT Docs, Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS", state: Done, assignee: Chloe Vance, description confirms Swift Relocations as carrier (PHMSA #3281074), 4 rack-mounted servers with lithium UPS, move window April 21–23, all checklist items marked VERIFIED including chain-of-custody/placarding; (b) linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 (#482), "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations", assignee: David Chen, references Victor Huang's server lab email, Chloe's timeline analysis, missing custody log, and Julian Brooks's note about Emilia Cruz's same-day callback request with no owner (piano damage details appear in Linear comments on this issue rather than in the issue description itself). The agent should note the contradiction: the DOT ticket is marked "Done" with chain-of-custody verified, but the actual execution had a chain-of-custody breach.
- Look up contact details for Marcus Thorne using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne" or "Marcus" or similar) to get marcus.thorne@moveops.com for the calendar event.
- Look up contact details for Catalina Dubois using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Catalina Dubois" or "Catalina" or similar) to get catalina.dubois@moveops.com for the calendar event.
- Correct QuickBooks entries based on audit findings. At minimum, the agent should flag or adjust the NorthWind invoice INV-2026-0412 surcharges: the $1,200 chain-of-custody charge is for a service that was not properly delivered (movers loaded before escort, equipment stored in prohibited warehouse overnight, confirmed by Victor Huang escalation and Chloe's timeline), and the $2,200 piano surcharge needs reconciliation against the $1,200 KeyMove insurance claim rider for the Steinway damage. The agent may create a corrected invoice (via quickbooks_create_invoice) or add notes/adjustments to reflect the audit findings. Any PENDING surcharges across other April invoices (GreenStack $1,650 storage, Mosaic $1,200/$800/$450, Canopy $850/$1,200/$600) should also be noted as requiring client approval before finalization.
- Update Airtable relocation records with audit findings using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01"). At minimum, the agent should update: (a) Emilia Cruz's record (recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver) with a note about the piano damage (Steinway scratched ~4.5–5 inches on bass side rim during stairwell extraction, KeyMove bill KM-44192-ICR filed for $1,200 insurance claim rider); (b) Raj Patel's record (recRajPatelChicagoDenver) with a note about the chain-of-custody breach (movers loaded at 7:00 AM before 9:00 AM escort, overnight warehouse storage, Victor Huang escalation, $1,200 surcharge under review).
- Create Linear tickets for unresolved vendor items using linear_create_issue. The agent should create at minimum: (a) A ticket for the KeyMove piano damage claim, the Steinway scratch needs resolution regarding MoveOps vs KeyMove liability, the $1,200 insurance claim rider has been billed but no resolution or settlement is documented; (b) A ticket for the SentinelChain chain-of-custody service failure, the breach (pre-escort loading, overnight warehouse storage) needs formal vendor follow-up and potential credit or surcharge reversal. Additional tickets may be warranted for pending surcharges requiring client approval across other April invoices.
- Schedule a calendar event on April 29, 2026 with Marcus Thorne and Catalina Dubois using calendar_add_calendar_event (start_datetime: "2026-04-29", attendees: marcus.thorne@moveops.com and catalina.dubois@moveops.com, title related to reviewing final NorthWind/April surcharge numbers before going to the client).
- Post audit findings in the #finance Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C005 or channel name "finance"). The message should summarize the surcharge audit results across all April invoices, highlighting: the NorthWind chain-of-custody surcharge issue ($1,200 for a botched service), the Emilia Cruz piano damage and $1,200 KeyMove insurance claim, PENDING surcharges across other clients requiring approval, and the April 29 meeting with Marcus and Catalina to finalize numbers.
